status_bar.upnp_tooltip: UPnP active - port 38291 mapped on router, inbound peers enabled

node_status.offline_banner_text: Node is offline. Start the node to use this page.

errors.boundary_message: An unexpected error occurred while rendering this page. The error has been logged.

language_selector.label: Display language
language_selector.description: Choose the language for menus, labels, and messages. Persists across restarts. Node and miner log output remain in English.

fields.currency_description: How to show Irium amounts throughout the app
fields.minimize_description: Keep node running when the window is closed
fields.rpc_url_description: URL for your local iriumd instance
fields.auto_start_description: Launch iriumd automatically when Irium Core opens
fields.wallet_path_description: Custom path to your wallet file (leave blank for default)
fields.data_dir_description: Custom iriumd data directory (leave blank for default)
fields.node_mode_description: Local runs the bundled iriumd. Remote points the GUI at a node you manage elsewhere (VPS, dedicated rig) — bundled miners stay enabled and connect via the URL above.
fields.rpc_token_description_local: Optional. Used as the Bearer token for the GUI's outbound RPC calls. Leave blank to use the auto-minted local token.
fields.rpc_token_description_remote: Required. Bearer token configured on the remote iriumd (its IRIUM_RPC_TOKEN environment variable).

toasts.latest_version: You are on the latest version
toasts.node_up_to_date: Node source is up to date
toasts.type_reset_to_confirm: Type RESET in the field to confirm
toasts.clear_state_failed: Failed to clear chain state — check node is stopped and try again
toasts.chain_state_cleared: Chain state cleared — restarting node from scratch…
toasts.upnp_failed_router: UPnP failed — router may not support it or port is already mapped
toasts.onboarding_reset: Onboarding reset — restart the app to see the wizard
toasts.update_check_internet: Could not check for updates. Check your internet connection.

settings.page_subtitle: Configure your node connection, wallet paths, and display preferences.

update_banner.title_available: A new version of Irium Core is available: v{{version}}
update_banner.sub_installed: Restart the app to start using the new version.
update_banner.open_releases: Open releases page in browser instead

connected_peers.title: Connected Peers
connected_peers.empty: No peers connected yet. Start the node to discover peers.
connected_peers.privacy_note: Peer IPs are only stored locally in your app and are never sent anywhere. Toggle off to hide them again.

port_reachability.title: Port Reachability
port_reachability.detecting_paragraph: Waiting up to 60s for an inbound peer to confirm reachability. PEX needs a moment to propagate your address to other nodes after start.
port_reachability.inactive_paragraph: UPnP failed and no inbound peers have arrived. Configure manual port forwarding on your router (TCP 38291) or enable UPnP, then click Retry. Outbound connections still work in this state.

security_info.wallet_local: Irium Core stores your wallet file locally. Your private keys never leave your machine. The wallet file is encrypted. Always back up your seed phrase and keep it offline.
security_info.rpc_token_warning: Token-based authentication is supported. To use IRIUM_RPC_TOKEN, paste the token into the RPC Token field in the Node Connection section above; the Test Remote button will verify it before you switch to Remote mode.

diagnostics.description: Runs 7 checks against your node, wallet, and binary setup.

about_info.check_github_releases: Check GitHub releases for a newer GUI version.

node_source_info.description_before_link: The node binaries (iriumd, irium-wallet, irium-miner) are compiled directly from the
node_source_info.description_after_link: repository, which is embedded as a git submodule. No features are ever missed — every capability added to the irium repo is automatically available to this app.

node_data_info.external_ip_description: Your public IP address announced to the network so peers can dial back to your node. Nothing is sent automatically — you control when detection happens.
node_data_info.detect_panel_paragraph: Enter a URL that returns your public IP as plain text. The request will only be sent when you click Fetch.
node_data_info.clear_state_description: Wipe block data and resync from scratch. Wallet and agreements are preserved.
node_data_info.confirm_hint: Click again to confirm. Blocks will be re-downloaded.
node_data_info.reset_state_description: Lighter alternative to Clear & Resync. Keeps your downloaded blocks and rebuilds the UTXO set locally. Use this when you see transaction signature failures.
node_data_info.reset_state_confirm_body: This renames your state folder to a timestamped backup and keeps your downloaded blocks. iriumd will rebuild the UTXO set from local blocks on next start — typically 5 to 15 minutes versus hours for a full chain resync. Use this when you see transaction signature failures or other UTXO-state corruption. Your wallet and agreements are not affected.
node_data_info.reset_state_no_backup: No existing state to back up — fresh state created
node_data_info.reset_state_rebuilding: Node restarting — rebuilding UTXO set from local blocks (5-15 min)

developer_info.reset_onboarding_description: Show the first-run setup wizard again on next launch
developer_info.show_legacy_settlement_description: Use the original template-grid Settlement page instead of the new SafeTrade / PayForWork / Deposit flows. Takes effect on next /settlement navigation.

action_bar.wallet_custom_warning_before: Your wallet is at a custom path:
action_bar.wallet_custom_warning_after: . After reset you will need to re-import it. Type RESET to confirm.

node_update_modal.title: Update Node Source
node_update_modal.body: This will pull the latest iriumd source code and rebuild the node binary. If the new code is unstable it may break your running node and require a restart. Only proceed if you know what you are doing.

operation_banner.completed_subtitle: Node is online and synced with the network.
operation_banner.slow_connect_hint: This may take a moment if no peers are immediately available. The node is actively trying to reach the Irium network.

banners.start_to_sync: Start the node to sync with the Irium network.
banners.peer_search_subtitle: Block #{{height}} · iriumd is discovering the network. If stuck, try Clear & Restart.
banners.sync_stalled_subtitle: Your node is behind the network. Click Clear & Restart to delete local chain data and resync from the network.
banners.height_stuck_subtitle: If your node appears stuck, try restarting or clearing the chain state.
banners.post_import_title: Wallet imported — syncing in the background
banners.post_import_body: Your wallet is restored. Your balance will appear here once iriumd finishes downloading the chain. This typically takes a few hours on a fresh install.
banners.height_warning: Block height hasn't changed in {{minutes}} minutes

decentral.title: Your node isn't reachable by the network
decentral.body: You're connected outbound but other nodes can't dial back to you. For a healthy decentralized network every node should be a full two-way peer. Set your public IP so the network knows how to reach you.
decentral.subtle_hint: Sync and wallet work without this

wallet.no_wallet_loaded: No wallet loaded — create or import one below
wallet.syncing_tooltip: Wait for the node to finish syncing before sending. Sending now can fail with a signature verification error because the UTXO set is still being rebuilt.

encryption.migration_body: Your wallet is currently stored unencrypted on this device. Set a password to protect it. The same addresses and balances will be preserved; nothing on-chain changes.
encryption.migration_seed_note: If you lose this password, recover with your seed phrase. The password only protects this device's wallet file.
encryption.password_too_short: Password must be at least 4 characters
encryption.unlock_body: Enter your wallet password to continue.
encryption.wrong_password: Wrong password — try again or recover with your seed phrase.
encryption.needs_migration_status: Wallet on disk uses the legacy unencrypted format and must be encrypted before use.
encryption.plaintext_backup_warning: Plaintext backup file(s) detected at {{paths}}. Delete after verifying the migration succeeded.
encryption.recover_link: Forgot your password? Recover with seed phrase

addresses.hide_address: Hide address (stays in wallet file, can be unhidden later)
addresses.empty_state: No addresses yet. Open Manage to add one.
addresses.add_address_confirm_body: This will derive the next address from your wallet seed. Addresses cannot be removed from the wallet file once created — only hidden from the visible list.
addresses.panel_description: Addresses are derived from your wallet seed and cannot be permanently deleted. Use Hide to remove them from view.

send.title: Send IRM
send.largest_first_hint: Picks bigger inputs first — smaller transaction, lower fee. Default is smallest-first, which drains dust over time.
send.security_note: Transactions are secured by your private key signature, not mining. No PoW required to send.
send.addr_invalid_prefix: Address must start with Q or P

errors.insufficient: You do not have enough IRM to complete this transaction. Check your balance and try a smaller amount.
errors.unconfirmed: Your funds are not yet confirmed. Wait for at least 1 confirmation before sending.
errors.double_spend: This transaction conflicts with another pending transaction. Please wait and try again.
errors.generic: Transaction could not be broadcast. Please check that the node is online and try again.
errors.insufficient_for_fee: Insufficient funds for the network fee. Address balance {{balance}} sats; minimum fee {{fee}} sats.
errors.node_still_syncing: Node is still syncing ({{persisted}}/{{height}} blocks). Please close this dialog, wait until syncing completes, and try again.
errors.not_accepted_mempool: Transaction was not accepted by the node mempool

send.node_offline_tooltip: Node must be online to send
send.node_syncing_tooltip: Node is syncing — please wait before sending
send.no_spendable_in_wallet: This wallet has no spendable funds.
send.transaction_sent_subtitle: Your transaction will appear in the next block
send.transaction_sent_explanation: Your transaction is cryptographically signed with your private key and broadcast to the network. It will be permanently secured when included in the next mined block.

receive.title: Receive IRM
receive.scan_qr_hint: Scan this QR code or copy the address below

security.seed_modal_warning: Write this down on paper. Anyone with these 24 words can spend your funds.
security.backup_warning: This file contains all your private keys. Store it securely.

toasts.node_must_be_online: Node must be online to send transactions
toasts.no_seed_backup: This wallet has no seed backup — use WIF Key or Export Backup File instead
toasts.restored_backup_with_restart: Wallet restored. Node restarted to pick up the new wallet state.
toasts.restored_backup_manual_restart: Wallet restored, but the node could not be restarted automatically. Restart it from the Dashboard so the new wallet is picked up.
toasts.no_recovery_data: No recovery data found in wallet

seed.recovery_phrase_warning: Never share your recovery phrase. Anyone with these 24 words can access all your funds. Store it offline securely.
seed.wallet_seed_warning: Never share your wallet seed. This hex value gives full access to all funds. Store it offline securely.
seed.custom_seed_hint: This wallet uses a custom seed. Back up this hex seed to restore.

restore.title: Restore from Backup
restore.warning_body: This will overwrite your current wallet data. Make sure you have your WIF key or backup file before continuing. The node will restart automatically once the restore completes so the new wallet state is picked up cleanly.

create_modal.generate_description: Generate a new BIP39 wallet with a 24-word recovery phrase.
create_modal.save_warning: Save your 24-word recovery phrase offline — it can restore your full wallet. Your WIF key backs up a single address. Neither can be recovered if lost.
create_modal.saved_confirmation: I have saved my recovery phrase and WIF key securely.
create_modal.import_notice_lead: This will import a new wallet. Your current wallet remains untouched and can be switched back from
create_modal.bip39_hint: BIP39 recovery phrase separated by spaces.
create_modal.wif_format_hint: Wallet Import Format — starts with 5, K, or L.

new_address.title: New Address
new_address.derived_lead: A fresh address has been derived from your wallet's BIP32 seed. Your
new_address.derived_trail: — this address shares the same wallet file as your existing addresses.
new_address.wif_warning: Anyone with this WIF can spend funds at this address. Store it offline.
new_address.recovery_reminder_lead: This address shares your wallet recovery phrase. To view it, go to

files.section_description: Each file has its own seed. Switch to load a different wallet.
files.create_new_description: Creates a separate wallet file with its own seed. Your current wallet remains on disk and can be switched back to from this list.

hidden.description: Addresses cannot be permanently deleted from the wallet file. Hidden addresses are removed from view but remain recoverable — click Unhide to bring them back.

delete_file.warning_funds_lead: This wallet contains funds. Deleting it without a backup means permanent loss of
delete_file.warning_funds_trail: . Make sure you have exported your WIF keys or seed phrase before proceeding.
delete_file.warning_unknown_lead: Balance could not be confirmed — the node may be offline. If this wallet held funds and you have not backed up the seed phrase or WIF keys, deletion will
delete_file.warning_zero_lead: This will delete the file from disk. If you have not backed up the seed phrase or WIF keys for this wallet, all funds in it will be

hide_address.body_lead: The address stays in the wallet file — only the visible list is updated. You can
hide_address.body_trail: it later from the Hidden Addresses section.

proof_modal.title: Submit proof
proof_modal.attested_by_hint: Pre-filled with your selected wallet address.
proof_modal.evidence_placeholder: e.g. Payment of $50 received via bank transfer, reference #12345.
proof_modal.intro: Build a signed proof in-app, or upload one you already have.
proof_modal.upload_note_prefix: Use this tab only if you already have a proof file — for example, one a counterparty or attestor signed for you, or one you produced via
proof_modal.upload_note_middle: If you are submitting your own proof, use the
proof_modal.upload_note_suffix: tab above — it builds and submits the proof without leaving the app.

toasts.invoice_parse_warning: Imported, but could not parse invoice fields for prefill

tooltips.import_invoice: Import a payment invoice JSON and create an agreement from it
tooltips.you_are_buyer: Your selected wallet address is this agreement's buyer
tooltips.you_are_seller: Your selected wallet address is this agreement's seller
tooltips.resolver_required: A resolver attestation is required to settle this dispute. Contact your designated resolver.
tooltips.deadline_passed: This agreement's deadline has passed without resolution.
tooltips.share_text: Copy a plain-text summary of this agreement to clipboard
tooltips.delete_local: Delete this agreement from your local store. The counterparty's copy is not affected.
tooltips.save_friendly_name: Save a friendly name for this agreement (stored locally)
tooltips.verify_signature_file: Verify a signature file (.sig or .json) against this agreement
tooltips.cleanup_old_test_data: Remove agreements with IDs containing test / gossip / demo / pr from your local store
tooltips.node_offline_sign: Node must be online to sign
tooltips.sign_to_confirm: Sign this agreement to confirm your participation
tooltips.node_offline_fund: Node must be online to fund escrow
tooltips.fund_escrow_action: Lock the agreement amount into the HTLC escrow
tooltips.node_offline_release: Node must be online to release funds
tooltips.node_offline_dispute: Node must be online to open a dispute
tooltips.mark_disputed: Mark this agreement as disputed — requires a resolver attestation to settle
tooltips.node_offline_refund: Node must be online to refund

agreements.search_placeholder: Search by ID, buyer/seller address, or label…

placeholders.dispute_reason: Describe what went wrong — this is shared with the resolver.

modals.delete_confirm_body: This removes the agreement from your local store only. The counterparty's copy is not affected. Only unfunded (open/pending) agreements can be deleted.
modals.cleanup_found: Found {{count}} agreement(s) with an id containing test, gossip, demo, or pr.
modals.cleanup_disclaimer: This removes them from your local store only. The counterparty's copy is not affected. Active on-chain HTLCs are not touched — funds still need to be released or refunded separately.
modals.fund_escrow_body_suffix: into the HTLC escrow. Funds release to the counterparty on proof acceptance, or refund after the timeout height.
modals.import_invoice_subtitle: Pick an invoice JSON to pre-fill a new agreement on the Settlement page.

empty.no_signatures: No signatures verified yet. Use the button above to verify one.
empty.disputes_offline: (Node is offline — dispute list may be unavailable.)
empty.go_to_settlement_cta: Go to Settlement to create your first agreement →

warnings.proof_finality_body: Wait for more block confirmations before releasing — a chain reorg could roll the proof back.
warnings.open_dispute_body: Opening a dispute means the agreement needs a resolver to review the evidence from both sides. Make sure you have a good reason and supporting proof before proceeding. This action cannot be undone.

helpers.sign_confirms_participation: Signing confirms your participation. Both parties must sign before funding.
helpers.sign_modal_body: Signing this agreement with your wallet key confirms your participation. Both parties must sign before funding.
helpers.no_hash_on_file: No hash on file for this agreement — paste the hash shared by the counterparty.
helpers.secret_required_hint: Required if you did not create this agreement yourself. Leave blank if your wallet auto-derives it.
helpers.use_invoice_hint: Clicking Use This Invoice jumps to Settlement with the OTC template pre-filled. You can change the template, edit fields, and confirm before creating the agreement.
helpers.signer_not_party: This address is not the buyer or seller — signing will be recorded without a party role.

form_errors.evidence_required: Describe what happened in the Evidence Summary
form_errors.secret_must_be_64_hex: Must be exactly 64 hex characters (0-9, a-f).

disputes.agreement_not_in_list: Agreement not in current list — try refreshing the Agreements view

settlement.page_subtitle: Trustless on-chain settlements using Irium's proof-based escrow system

hub.empty_state: You haven't created any agreements yet.
hub.selling_subtitle: Create an offer, share with buyer, receive payment on proof
hub.buying_subtitle: Find an offer, fund escrow, release payment on delivery

templates_desc.otc: Use this to buy or sell IRM peer-to-peer. The buyer locks IRM in escrow, makes an off-chain payment, and the seller confirms receipt to release funds.
templates_desc.freelance: Use this for freelance work or services. The client locks payment in escrow upfront. Funds release when the contractor completes the work and submits proof.
templates_desc.milestone: Use this for large projects split into stages. Each milestone has its own escrow and proof requirement. Partial releases happen as milestones complete.
templates_desc.deposit: Use this for refundable deposits or collateral. Funds lock on-chain and return automatically if conditions are not met by the deadline.
templates_desc.merchant_delayed: Merchant sale with a built-in cool-down window. Funds are held in escrow and the merchant can claim only after the dispute window closes.
templates_desc.contractor: Contractor work split into milestones. Each milestone has its own escrow and deliverable proof requirement. Funds release stage by stage.

form_errors.same_party: Party A and Party B cannot be the same address
form_errors.deadline_range: Deadline must be between 1 and {{max}} hours (1 year)
form_errors.cooldown_range: Cool-down must be between 1 and {{max}} hours (1 year)

preview.fill_hint: Fill the form to see a live preview

dashboard.select_seller_hint: Select a wallet address to view seller stats.
dashboard.select_buyer_hint: Select a wallet address to view buyer stats.
dashboard.total_received_hint: Lifetime volume of released agreements where you were the seller (sats → IRM)
dashboard.total_spent_hint: Lifetime volume of released agreements where you were the buyer (sats → IRM)

errors.generic_failure: Something went wrong. Please check your node is running and try again.

hub.title: Settlement
hub.subtitle: Lock IRM in escrow. Release on confirmation. Refund on timeout.
hub.safe_trade_subtitle: Lock IRM, swap for anything. Both sides protected.
hub.pay_for_work_subtitle: Fund a job in one payment or by milestone. Money releases when work is done.
hub.deposit_subtitle: Lock IRM as a guarantee. Get it back automatically if conditions aren't met.
hub.active_empty: No active agreements yet. Start one above.
hub.active_load_error: Couldn't load agreements — your node may be offline.
hub.toast_released: Funds released to the other party.
hub.toast_refunded: Refund claimed — funds are returning to your wallet.
hub.intro_security: All settlements are secured by IRM locked on-chain. Both parties must hold IRM to create or participate in an agreement.
hub.intro_use_cases: Lock IRM in escrow for any trustless transaction — goods, services, freelance work, or trades. IRM releases automatically when both parties confirm.

address.format_error: This doesn't look like a valid Irium address.

deal_code.paste_placeholder: Paste the trade code your seller sent you here…
deal_code.fallback_explainer: Couldn't build a deal code. Share this agreement ID instead — your buyer's node will need to be online to fetch the rest.

errors.generic: Something went wrong — please try again.
errors.node_offline: Your Irium node is not running. Start it from the Dashboard, then try again.
errors.network_timeout: The request took too long. Check your connection and try again.
errors.auth_failed: Your node rejected the request. Check your RPC token in Settings.
errors.insufficient_funds: Not enough IRM in your wallet for this action.
errors.coinbase_immature: Some of your IRM came from recent mining and isn't spendable yet. Try again after a few more blocks.
errors.double_spend: Another pending transaction is using the same funds. Wait a moment, then try again.
errors.already_settled: This agreement is already settled. Refresh to see its current state.
errors.refund_not_ready: You can't claim a refund yet. Wait until the deadline passes.
errors.release_not_ready: Funds aren't ready to release yet. Wait for proof confirmation.
errors.agreement_mismatch: This deal code doesn't match the agreement on your node. Ask the other party to resend it.
errors.bad_secret: The release key is wrong. Check that you have the right deal code.
errors.bad_address: That address doesn't look right. Double-check and try again.
errors.bad_format: The data couldn't be read. It may be corrupted or from a different app.
errors.fs_blocked: The app couldn't save the deal code locally. Check your disk permissions.
errors.file_missing: Couldn't find the saved deal code file.
errors.pack_not_found: We couldn't find this agreement on your node.
errors.unpack_signature_invalid: The deal code is signed wrong. Don't trust it — ask the other party to resend.
errors.dispute_duplicate: A dispute is already open for this agreement.
errors.dispute_not_party: Only the buyer or seller can raise a dispute on this trade.

safe_trade.step1_title: Which side of the trade are you on?
safe_trade.step1_subtitle: Pick the side that matches your role.
safe_trade.selling_subtitle: You're locking IRM in escrow and waiting for payment.
safe_trade.buying_subtitle: You're paying off-chain and receiving IRM.
safe_trade.status_initial: Setting up — your funds are being locked.
safe_trade.status_waiting_verify: Waiting for your buyer to verify the deal.
safe_trade.status_waiting_payment: Buyer verified — waiting for their off-chain payment.
safe_trade.status_ready_release: Payment confirmed. Releasing funds to your buyer now.
safe_trade.toast_released: Funds released to the other party.
safe_trade.toast_release_pending: Payment recorded. Funds will release after the next few blocks confirm.

errors.amount_required: Enter how much IRM you want to lock.
errors.receiving_required: Tell your buyer what you're expecting in return.
errors.fund_after_create_failed: Agreement created but funding failed. Open the Agreements page to retry.
errors.payment_ref_required: Enter a transaction reference so the seller can verify your payment.

review.deadline_value: If not paid in {{hours}} hours, your IRM returns automatically.
review.tx_note: This will create a transaction on the Irium blockchain.
review.tx_broadcast_note: A blockchain transaction will be broadcast. This may take a moment to confirm.

share.title: Share this with your buyer
share.subtitle: Send the code to your buyer. They'll use it to verify the trade before paying you off-chain.

selling.step2_subtitle: Tell us who your buyer is and how much you're locking.
selling.step4_title: Funds locked — share this with your buyer
selling.step4_subtitle: Send the deal code to your buyer. They'll use it to verify the trade before paying you.
selling.amount_label: How much IRM are you locking?
selling.amount_helper: Held in escrow until you confirm you received payment.
selling.deadline_label: How long should this trade stay open?
selling.deadline_helper: If they don't pay within this time, your IRM returns automatically.
selling.release_button: I received payment — Release funds to buyer
selling.buyer_address_helper: The address that receives the IRM when you confirm the buyer paid.
selling.amount_helper_short: Held in escrow until you confirm the buyer's payment.
selling.receiving_placeholder_detailed: e.g. 50 USDT via SEPA bank transfer
selling.deadline_label_short: Deadline for the buyer to pay
selling.deadline_helper_refund: If no payment arrives by the deadline you can claim a refund.
selling.locked_hero_title: Your IRM is now locked and protected
selling.locked_hero_body: The buyer cannot take it without your confirmation. Share the deal code below so they can join, then wait for their payment.
selling.share_code_body: The buyer pastes this code in their wallet to join the trade.
selling.header1_subtitle: Tell us who the buyer is and how much IRM you want to sell.
selling.header2_subtitle: Confirm the details. Locking sends the IRM to escrow.
selling.header3_subtitle: Share the deal code with the buyer and wait for their payment.

buying.step2_title: Paste the trade code from your seller
buying.step2_subtitle: Your seller sent you a code. Paste it below to verify the trade.
buying.step3_subtitle: Make sure the details match what you agreed off-chain.
buying.step4_subtitle: Send the agreed payment to your seller. Your IRM will arrive automatically once they confirm.
buying.review_intro: This trade has been imported and verified on your node. The seller's IRM is locked in escrow — you'll receive it when you've sent off-chain payment and the seller confirms.
buying.details_unavailable: Couldn't fetch trade details from your node. You can still proceed, but verify the agreement ID matches what your seller sent you.
buying.confirm_button: This looks right — I'll send payment
buying.status_waiting: Waiting for the seller to confirm payment received.
buying.status_releasing: Payment confirmed — IRM is on its way.
buying.status_complete: Complete — IRM has arrived in your wallet.
buying.review_commit_intro: Confirm the trade details below. By continuing, you commit to this trade. After this step, send the off-chain payment to the seller and report it from the next screen.
buying.details_did_not_load: Trade details did not load. You can still continue, but verify the IRM amount with the seller before paying.
buying.trade_complete_body: The IRM has arrived in your wallet.
buying.seller_locked_for_you_title: The seller's IRM is locked for you
buying.seller_locked_for_you_body: Send the off-chain payment to the seller, then report your transaction reference below.
buying.report_payment_body: After you send the off-chain payment, paste any reference the seller can use to confirm it arrived - bank wire reference, PayPal transaction id, etc.
buying.report_payment_placeholder: Bank wire reference, PayPal txid, blockchain txid, etc.
buying.header1_subtitle: The seller will have sent you a code after they locked their IRM.
buying.header2_subtitle: Make sure the amount and seller match what you expect.
buying.header3_subtitle: Send the off-chain payment, then report it so the seller can release the IRM.

safe_trade.fuel_banner_title: You need IRM to create a settlement.
safe_trade.toast_buyer_linked: Your side is now linked to the trade. Send the off-chain payment to the seller.
safe_trade.toast_payment_reported: Payment reported. Waiting for the seller to confirm.
safe_trade.toast_dispute_opened: Dispute opened. A resolver will review the evidence.
safe_trade.choose_selling_body: Lock your IRM in escrow. The buyer pays you off-chain, then you release.
safe_trade.choose_buying_body: Paste the seller's deal code. Send them payment, then confirm to receive IRM.

pay_for_work.single_subtitle: Pay everything upfront when the work is done.
pay_for_work.milestone_subtitle: Pay in stages as each part is completed.
pay_for_work.step2_subtitle_single: Tell us who you're paying and how much.
pay_for_work.step2_subtitle_milestone: Tell us the total, the number of stages, and what's expected.
pay_for_work.step4_subtitle: Waiting for the work to be delivered.
pay_for_work.amount_helper_milestone: Split equally across the milestones below.
pay_for_work.deadline_helper: How long until the funds auto-refund if work isn't confirmed.

errors.amount_required: Enter how much IRM you're locking.

pay_for_work.status_setting_up: Setting up — funds are being locked.
pay_for_work.status_pending: Waiting for funding to confirm on-chain.
pay_for_work.status_in_progress: Funds locked. Waiting for work to be delivered.
pay_for_work.status_can_refund: Deadline reached. You can claim a refund.
pay_for_work.toast_release_pending: Completion recorded. Funds will release after the next few blocks confirm.
pay_for_work.toast_refunded: Refund claimed — funds are returning to your wallet.
pay_for_work.toast_fund_failed: Agreement created but funding failed. Find it in your Agreements page to retry funding.
pay_for_work.milestone_helper: Mark Complete releases this milestone's share of the locked IRM. The contractor receives 1/{{count}} of the total per milestone.

deposit.step1_subtitle: Lock IRM as a guarantee. Get it back automatically if conditions aren't met.
deposit.step3_subtitle: Held in escrow until you release it, or until the deadline passes and you claim it back.
deposit.recipient_label: Who receives it if conditions are met?
deposit.purpose_helper: A note for your records. Stored locally — the other party may not see this.
deposit.deadline_helper: If the recipient doesn't claim it by this deadline, you can claim it back.

errors.amount_required: Enter how much IRM you're depositing.

deposit.status_setting_up: Setting up — your deposit is being locked.
deposit.status_locked: Locked in escrow. Will release on your action, or auto-refund after the deadline.
deposit.status_can_claim_back: Deadline reached. You can claim the deposit back.
deposit.toast_refunded: Deposit claimed back — funds are returning to your wallet.

dispute.step1_subtitle: Describe the problem in your own words. The other party will see this.
dispute.step2_subtitle: Your dispute is now on the Irium blockchain.
dispute.reason_placeholder: What went wrong? Be specific — the other party will read this.
dispute.evidence_coming_soon: File attachments are coming in a future release.
dispute.confirm_title: Your dispute has been recorded on-chain.
dispute.confirm_body: The other party has been notified. Contact them directly to work out a resolution. If you can't reach an agreement, the next step is an off-app conversation — automated referee resolution is coming in a future release.
dispute.next_step_note: While the dispute is open, neither side can release or refund the funds. Talk to your counterparty before anything else.

errors.no_agreement: No agreement selected. Open one from the hub and try again.

marketplace.tagline: Browse and post settlement offers on the Irium peer-to-peer network.

create_offer.title: Create Sell Offer
create_offer.how_works_step_1: You create this offer (the IRM is locked in escrow now).
create_offer.how_works_step_2: A buyer funds the escrow with their IRM share.
create_offer.how_works_step_3: Buyer pays you off-chain using the instructions below.
create_offer.how_works_step_5: Escrow releases the IRM to the buyer.
create_offer.escrow_note: On-chain escrow protects both sides — neither of you can disappear with the money.

fields.amount_hint: This amount will be deducted from your wallet and locked in escrow.
fields.seller_address_placeholder: Optional · leave blank for wallet default
fields.seller_address_hint: Pre-filled from your selected wallet address. Must be an address whose key your wallet holds.
fields.min_trade_placeholder: e.g. 0.1 (buyers cannot take less than this)
fields.min_trade_hint: Leave blank if you'll accept any size. Communicated to buyers via the offer description.
fields.payment_method_placeholder: e.g. Bank Transfer, PayPal, USDT, BTC, Cash
fields.payment_method_hint: Short label that helps buyers filter offers — pick the rail they should use.
fields.payment_details_label: Your payment details (what to send to you)
fields.payment_details_hint: The buyer sees these instructions after the escrow is funded. Be specific — wrong details mean lost time.

create_offer.confirm_large_amount:
You're about to lock {{amount}} IRM in escrow.

This is a large amount. Confirm you typed the decimal correctly.
create_offer.remote_tooltip: This offer is from another seller and cannot be deleted from your local store.
create_offer.delete_tooltip: Delete this offer from your local store
create_offer.modal_subtitle: Post an offer to the order book — IRM locks the moment a buyer takes it.
create_offer.lock_info_prefix: Your IRM is locked the moment a buyer commits. You then receive the buyer's off-chain payment and click
create_offer.lock_info_suffix: to release. Or open a dispute if they don't pay.
create_offer.payment_details_placeholder: e.g. IBAN DE89 3704 0044 0532 0130 00 or PayPal: seller@example.com
create_offer.payment_details_helper: The buyer sees these details after they take the offer.

errors.positive_usdt_price: Enter a positive USDT price per IRM
errors.payment_details_required: Enter your payment details so the buyer knows how to pay

toasts.offer_posted: Offer posted. IRM will lock the moment a buyer commits.

take_offer.title: Take Offer
take_offer.error_agreement_id_missing: Offer taken but agreement id missing in response
take_offer.irm_locked_send_payment: IRM is now locked in escrow. Send the off-chain payment.
take_offer.dispute_opened_refund_hint: Dispute opened. You can claim your refund from the Agreements page after the timeout.
take_offer.enter_payment_reference: Enter a transaction id or note so the seller can verify your payment
take_offer.payment_reported_seller_notified: Payment reported. The seller has been notified.
take_offer.step1_intro: Confirming locks the seller's IRM in escrow. You then have a deadline to send the off-chain payment and report the transaction.
take_offer.step2_intro: The seller's IRM is locked. Send the off-chain payment, then enter your transaction reference below and click "I have sent payment".
take_offer.no_payment_details: No details in the offer. Contact the seller directly for payment instructions.
take_offer.payment_reference_placeholder: Bank wire reference, PayPal txid, blockchain txid, etc.
take_offer.payment_reference_hint: The seller sees this exactly. Anything they can use to confirm your payment arrived works - reference number, transaction id, sender name, etc.
take_offer.cancel_opens_dispute_warning: If you cancel here, a dispute opens. You can claim a refund after the timeout from the Agreements page.

feeds.discover_tooltip: Ask the wallet binary to discover seller feeds on the network

browse.remote_filter_warning: You are viewing remote offers only. Your own local offers are hidden by this filter.
browse.auto_shared_hint: Your offers are automatically shared with connected peers via P2P. No manual setup needed.
browse.nat_faq_receiving_body: Always works. Your node fetches from publicly-reachable peers regardless of your own NAT status — any normal internet connection can browse the marketplace.
browse.nat_faq_sharing_body: Requires your node to be reachable. Peers fetch from http://<your-ip>:38300/offers/feed, which means a public IPv4 and an open port 38300. The desktop wallet attempts UPnP port-forwarding automatically when an external endpoint is detected.
browse.nat_faq_cgnat_body: You can still BUY offers from others. For others to see YOUR offers, options are: (a) request a public IPv4 from your ISP, (b) run iriumd on a VPS, or (c) export the offer with `irium-wallet offer-export` and share the JSON directly with your counterparty, who imports it with `offer-import`.

toasts.offer_created_browse_ready: Offer created successfully — visible in Browse tab
toasts.min_must_be_positive: Minimum trade amount must be a positive number
toasts.min_cannot_exceed: Minimum cannot exceed the offer amount
toasts.relisted_banner_body: Your offer was taken but the buyer did not fund the agreement. The offer has been relisted automatically.
toasts.take_failed_already_taken: This offer has already been taken by another buyer.

swap.fuel_banner_body: Swap BTC, LTC or DOGE for IRM to start trading trustlessly.
swap.pair_not_available: This pair is not available yet
swap.own_order_hint: That is your own order. Use the My Swaps panel on the right to cancel it.
swap.how_it_works_step1: Pick an order from the book on the left, or post your own.
swap.how_it_works_step2: The seller's IRM is locked in escrow the moment the order is taken.
swap.how_it_works_step3: The buyer sends {{quote}} to the seller's address.
swap.how_it_works_step4: Once the payment confirms, the IRM is released to the buyer automatically.

header.subtitle_otc: Peer-to-peer OTC. Lock IRM in escrow, swap for anything.
header.subtitle_swap: Atomic swaps between IRM and other chains. Trustless. No bridge.

my_trades.title: My Trades
my_trades.empty_active: No active trades. Take an offer from the order book to start.
my_trades.empty_all: No trades yet. Take an offer from the order book to start.

order_book.title: Order Book
order_book.hidden_tooltip_one: {{count}} offer hidden because it had no quoted price
order_book.hidden_tooltip_other: {{count}} offers hidden because they had no quoted price
order_book.hidden_tooltip: {{count}} offers hidden because they had no quoted price
order_book.empty_prefix: No offers in the book yet. Click

pair_order_book.empty_state: No open {{pair}} orders. Be the first — post one with the New swap button above.

resolver_picker.title: Resolver selection
resolver_picker.intro: A resolver is a verified third party who decides who gets the IRM when buyer and seller cannot agree. The resolver earns a small fee, deducted from the disputed amount when the trade settles.
resolver_picker.no_nominated_notice: This trade was created without nominated resolvers. Both parties can co-sign a fresh nomination from the wallet's resolver re-nomination flow before any resolver below can act.
resolver_picker.registry_unreachable: The resolver registry endpoint is not reachable on this node ({{error}}). Use the resolver re-nomination flow from your wallet to discover candidates directly via the resolver-list CLI.
resolver_picker.empty_registry_before: No resolvers have registered on this node yet. Resolvers register by calling
resolver_picker.empty_registry_after: after their address has appeared in a recent coinbase output.
resolver_picker.agreements_hint: Use the Agreements page to submit additional evidence to the resolver and to watch the dispute resolution unfold. Both parties can submit evidence at any time before the resolver decides.

seller_review.toast_released: IRM released. The buyer now has the funds.
seller_review.dispute_reason: seller did not receive off-chain payment
seller_review.toast_dispute_opened: Dispute opened. A resolver will review the evidence.
seller_review.empty_state: No incoming trades waiting on you. When a buyer takes one of your offers and reports payment, it shows up here.
seller_review.no_payment_yet: Buyer has not yet reported sending payment. Wait, or open a dispute if the deadline has passed.

take_swap.toast_no_wallet: No active Irium wallet address. Open a wallet first.
take_swap.toast_min_blocks: Refund deadline must be at least 10 blocks
take_swap.toast_locked_buyer: Order locked. Send the {{code}} payment and report it.
take_swap.toast_locked_seller: Order locked. The seller's IRM is in escrow.
take_swap.toast_need_ref: Enter a transaction id or note so the seller can verify your {{code}} payment
take_swap.toast_payment_reported: Payment reported. The seller has been notified.
take_swap.toast_dispute_info: A dispute can be opened from the Agreements page. Cancel here for now and revisit it there.
take_swap.intro_buyer: Confirming will lock the seller's IRM in escrow. You will then send {{code}} to the address shown next.
take_swap.intro_seller: Confirming will create a matching escrow with your IRM. The buyer will send {{code}} to your address.
take_swap.refund_deadline_hint: If payment is not confirmed by this deadline, your funds can be refunded.
take_swap.escrow_locked_buyer: Send the {{code}} payment to the address below. Once your payment confirms, the IRM is released to your wallet automatically.
take_swap.escrow_locked_seller: Your IRM is in escrow. The buyer will send {{code}} to your address. When their payment confirms, the IRM is released to them and the buyer flow finishes on its own.
take_swap.memo_hint: Most {{name}} wallets include this as an extra data field on the payment. It links your payment to this trade so the IRM can release automatically.
take_swap.tx_reference_hint: The seller sees this exactly. Anything they can use to confirm your payment works.
take_swap.cancel_warning: If you cancel now, your funds stay locked until the refund deadline above.

order_type_picker.title: What do you want to do?
order_type_picker.subtitle: Pick the way you'd like to trade — off-chain payment or trustless atomic swap.
order_type_picker.otc_description: Post an offer to sell IRM for off-chain payment (bank transfer, PayPal, USDT, SEPA, cash). Buyer locks escrow, sends payment off-chain, you confirm receipt to release.
order_type_picker.btc_description: Trustless atomic swap. IRM locks on-chain; BTC unlocks on Bitcoin via the same preimage. No counterparty risk, no off-chain payment step.
order_type_picker.ltc_description: Atomic swap via Litecoin SPV header relay. Same trust-minimised design as the BTC pair, settling on Litecoin's faster blocks.
order_type_picker.doge_description: Atomic swap via Dogecoin SPV header relay. Pair will go live as the network reaches the activation height.

unexpected_exit.title: Miner stopped unexpectedly
unexpected_exit.body_mac: Your miner stopped unexpectedly. This can happen on Mac due to GPU power limits or the macOS GPU watchdog. Click Restart Miner to resume.
unexpected_exit.body_other: Your miner stopped unexpectedly. Check the miner log for details, then click Restart Miner to resume.

fields.address_invalid_inline: Invalid address. Must start with P or Q

sync.warming_up_body: The miner sidecar has started. The first hashrate reading lands within 1-3 seconds — no action needed.

solo.subtitle: Run an ASIC against your own iriumd. No pool fee, no pool dependency.

welcome_screen.wizard_intro: This setup wizard will configure your node, sync the chain, and create or restore your wallet.

pool_stats.subtitle: Live status of pool.iriumlabs.org
pool_stats.load_error: Could not load pool stats — proxy may be unreachable
pool_stats.scanner_note: Connections shown include port scanners. Active miners are those with accepted shares.
pool_stats.col_rejected_15m_tooltip: Rejected shares in the last 15 minutes only. The cumulative count (since stratum restart) kept a worker showing a stale warmup-burst total even after they recovered.
pool_stats.col_reject_pct_15m_tooltip: Rolling rejection rate over the last 15 minutes. The cumulative-since-restart rate kept a worker stuck on a post-restart warmup burst even after they recovered.
pool_stats.col_blocks_tooltip: Blocks found by this worker at the pool. Populated by the stats-proxy; shows '—' until the proxy backfills per-worker block credit.

mining_overview.title: Network Mining Overview
mining_overview.subtitle: One-screen view of network, pool, and your own mining. Refreshes every 30 seconds.

founder_card.title: Founder Vesting Address
founder_card.description: This address holds 3,500,000 IRM locked via CLTV timelock at genesis. Balance shown as 0 because locked funds use a special script not counted as spendable balance.

solo_pool.title: Solo Pool
solo_pool.subtitle: Coinbase pays the full block reward directly to the block finder. Zero fees.
solo_pool.connect_here: Connect with your own Irium address as the worker name
solo_pool.connect_help: Paste this URL into your ASIC firmware. Use your Irium address as the worker username (Q-prefix) and any password.
solo_pool.no_miners: No solo miners connected yet — be the first.
solo_pool.load_error: Could not load solo pool stats — proxy may be unreachable

richlist.title: Top IRM Holders
richlist.subtitle: Ranked by spendable balance on the current chain tip
richlist.max_supply_tooltip: The Irium blockchain has a fixed maximum supply of 100,000,000 IRM. This is a protocol-enforced hard cap that can never be changed.
richlist.locked_note: Includes the 3,500,000 IRM founder allocation, locked on-chain by CLTV at genesis, plus any multisig (P-prefix) outputs from settlement agreements. These are accounted in total supply but are not P2PKH spendable, so they do not appear as individual rich-list entries. Auto-refreshes every 60 seconds.
richlist.you_note: The rich list ranks each address individually, so each "You" row only reflects that one address's balance. Your wallet may hold several addresses — the top bar shows the sum across all of them.
richlist.founder_vesting_tooltip: Founder allocation locked at genesis via CLTV timelock. Not spendable until the lock height is reached.
richlist.prefix_note: Both P and Q prefix addresses are standard single-signature wallet addresses using the same version byte 0x39. The leading character difference is a base58 encoding property of the underlying public key hash — not a type difference.
richlist.empty: No P2PKH balances on chain yet.
richlist.node_offline_hint: Start the node on the Dashboard to load the rich list.
richlist.sr_total_supply: {{total}} IRM total supply context for screen readers

specs_values.block_time: ~10 minutes target (at launch — see Dashboard for live network rate)
specs_values.block_reward: 50 IRM at launch (Early Miner Era — halves on schedule)
specs_values.key_derivation: BIP39 24-word mnemonic seed phrase + WIF key (both supported for backup/recovery)

about_app.intro: Irium Core is a full-node GUI desktop wallet for the Irium blockchain. It runs a complete iriumd node locally, giving you full sovereignty over your funds and settlement agreements. No third-party servers. No custodians. Your keys, your chain.

privacy.title: Your Data Stays on Your Machine
privacy.intro: Irium Core is a full-node desktop wallet that runs entirely on your computer. No data is collected, transmitted, or stored on any external server.
privacy.bullet_1: No analytics or telemetry — the app does not phone home
privacy.bullet_2: No account registration required — there are no user accounts
privacy.bullet_3: Wallet keys and seed phrases are stored locally in your ~/.irium/ directory only
privacy.bullet_4: Transaction history is read directly from your local blockchain copy
privacy.bullet_5: Peer connections use direct IP-to-IP communication with no intermediary servers
privacy.bullet_6: No DNS lookups — peer discovery is fully DNS-free using signed seedlists and blockchain-embedded addresses

report_issue.body: Found a bug or need help? Open an issue on the Irium Core GitHub repository. Include your app version, operating system, and a description of the problem.

licenses.title: Licenses
licenses.body: Irium Core is open source software released under the MIT License.

faqs.q1: Does Irium Core collect any data?
faqs.a1: No. Irium Core runs entirely on your computer. No analytics, no telemetry, no usage tracking. The app never contacts any external server except the Irium P2P network for blockchain synchronization. Your wallet keys, transaction history, and all settings stay on your local machine. There are no user accounts and no registration.
faqs.q2: Where are my wallet files stored?
faqs.a2: All wallet data is stored in your local ~/.irium/ directory (on Windows: C:\Users\YourName\.irium\). This includes your wallet.json file, blockchain data, peer cache, and configuration. Nothing is stored in the cloud. If you uninstall the app, your wallet files remain on disk — you can reinstall and pick up where you left off. Always back up your recovery phrase and WIF keys separately in case of hardware failure.
faqs.q3: My node is not connecting to peers
faqs.a3: Irium uses a DNS-free bootstrap system with signed seed nodes. If you are behind a strict firewall, ensure outbound TCP port 38291 is allowed. You can also add a peer manually from the Network Sync screen or in Settings using the Add Seed option.
faqs.a4: Go to the Mining page, enter your IRM address in the configuration field, set the number of threads, and click Start Mining. Your address must start with P or Q. Average block time varies with network hashrate — currently around 13 minutes; the Dashboard shows the live value.
faqs.a5: A settlement agreement locks IRM in escrow between a buyer and seller. Funds are only released when proof requirements are met or the timeout expires. Go to the Settlement page to create or manage agreements.
faqs.q6: My wallet shows 0 IRM but I received funds
faqs.a6: Click Refresh on the Wallet page or wait for the next polling cycle (15 seconds). If the balance still shows 0, ensure your node is fully synced — check the block height matches the network on the Block Explorer page.
faqs.q7: How do I back up my wallet?
faqs.a7: Irium wallets use BIP39 with a 24-word recovery phrase. To back up your wallet, go to Wallet → Security → Show Recovery Phrase to view your 24-word mnemonic, or Export WIF Key for a single-address backup. Store the recovery phrase or WIF key offline in a safe place — the recovery phrase restores your entire wallet, while a WIF key only restores one address.
faqs.q8: What is the difference between P and Q addresses?
faqs.a8: Both P and Q addresses are valid Irium addresses. They are derived from different parts of your HD wallet key tree. All addresses in your wallet belong to you and can receive IRM.
faqs.q9: How do I connect to the mining pool?
faqs.a9: Go to the Mining page and select the Stratum Pool tab. Enter the pool URL stratum+tcp://pool.iriumlabs.org:3333 for ASIC/modern miners or stratum+tcp://pool.iriumlabs.org:3335 for CPU/GPU miners. Your username should be your IRM address followed by a worker name, for example PwjVf7nY19UWW2i9HCEPyZt81975M6iKdW.worker1.
faqs.q10: macOS says Irium Core is damaged or quarantined. How do I fix it?
faqs.a10: macOS quarantines apps downloaded outside the App Store. To fix this: (1) open Terminal; (2) run: xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/Irium\ Core.app — this removes the quarantine flag from the entire app bundle. If you still see "damaged", run: codesign --force --deep --sign - /Applications/Irium\ Core.app then re-launch. The first launch may take 10-20 seconds while Gatekeeper re-verifies the signature. If iriumd itself was quarantined (you see "iriumd is damaged" in the logs), run: xattr -d com.apple.quarantine ~/.irium/binaries/iriumd to unquarantine just the node binary.
faqs.q11: My node has quarantined blocks. How do I recover?
faqs.a11: iriumd quarantines block files it cannot validate (typically after a crash mid-write or a corrupted reorg). Quarantined blocks live in ~/.irium/blocks/orphaned_<timestamp>/. You can clear them from the Quarantined Blocks Recovery section above (stop the node first). The node will re-fetch the missing blocks from peers automatically on next start. If the resync gets stuck at the same height, use Settings → Clear chain state for a clean resync from genesis (your wallet and agreements are preserved).
faqs.q12: I'm behind NAT or CGNAT. Will my marketplace offers be visible to others?
faqs.a12: The marketplace has two directions, with different reachability requirements. RECEIVING offers from others always works — your node fetches from publicly-reachable peers regardless of your own NAT status; any normal internet connection can browse the marketplace. SHARING your offers requires your node to be reachable: peers fetch from http://<your-ip>:38300/offers/feed, which means a public IPv4 and an open port 38300. The desktop wallet attempts UPnP port-forwarding automatically when an external endpoint is detected. If you're behind CGNAT or strict NAT and cannot expose port 38300, you can still BUY offers from others, but for them to see YOUR offers you can: (a) request a public IPv4 from your ISP, (b) run iriumd on a VPS, or (c) export the offer with `irium-wallet offer-export` and share the JSON directly — the counterparty imports it with `offer-import`.

port_forwarding.section_title: How to forward ports for better connectivity
port_forwarding.intro: Irium uses port 38291 for P2P connections. Forwarding this port lets other nodes connect to you directly, improving your network connectivity and mining performance.
port_forwarding.step1_windows: Windows: open Command Prompt, type ipconfig. Look for IPv4 Address (e.g. 192.168.1.100).
port_forwarding.step2_body: Open a browser and go to 192.168.1.1 or 192.168.0.1. Log in with your router username and password (usually printed on the router).
port_forwarding.step3_body: Look for: Port Forwarding, NAT, Virtual Server, or Applications (the name varies by router brand).
port_forwarding.step4_internal_ip: Internal IP: your local IP from step 1
port_forwarding.step5_body: The status bar at the bottom of the app shows UPnP when port forwarding is active. A green dot means connected peers were found.
port_forwarding.upnp_note: UPnP is enabled by default in Irium and will try to forward the port automatically. Manual port forwarding is only needed if UPnP is not supported by your router.
port_forwarding.result_closed: Port 38291 appears closed — try manual port forwarding above.

upnp_diag.title: UPnP diagnostics
upnp_diag.verdict_double_nat: Router accepted — but double NAT / CGNAT

quarantine.description: iriumd quarantines block files it cannot validate. They live in orphaned_<timestamp> folders inside your blocks directory. Clearing them is safe — the node re-fetches the affected heights from peers.
quarantine.status_count_one: Found {{files}} quarantined block file in {{dirs}} folder.
quarantine.status_count_other: Found {{files}} quarantined block files across {{dirs}} folders.
quarantine.confirm_body: This permanently deletes {{files}} file(s) across {{dirs}} folder(s). The node must be stopped before clearing. Continue?
quarantine.node_running_warning: Stop the node from the Dashboard before clearing quarantined blocks.

settlement_help.what_body: Settlement lets two parties lock funds in a provably-fair on-chain escrow. Money is only released when the recipient submits a cryptographic proof — or returned to the sender if the deadline passes. No intermediary, no disputes with a central authority.
settlement_help.templates_intro: Pick the template that matches your use-case:
settlement_help.template_otc_desc: direct asset swap between two parties.
settlement_help.template_freelance_desc: single-payment job escrow with optional scope description.
settlement_help.template_milestones_desc: split payment across 2–20 equal milestones, each requiring a proof.
settlement_help.template_timelock_desc: funds held until a deadline then released or refunded.
settlement_help.template_merchant_desc: buyer pays, merchant claims after a cool-down window that gives the buyer time to dispute.
settlement_help.template_contractor_desc: milestone escrow framed around client / contractor roles with optional scope.
settlement_help.creating_step_2: Fill in the buyer's address, amount, deadline, and any template-specific fields.
settlement_help.creating_step_3: Review the summary — verify addresses and amounts before confirming.
settlement_help.creating_step_4: Share the agreement ID with the buyer so they can fund it.
settlement_help.creating_step_5: Submit a delivery proof once you've fulfilled the obligation.
settlement_help.creating_step_6: Claim your funds after the proof is accepted.
settlement_help.funding_step_2: Paste the agreement ID the seller shared.
settlement_help.funding_step_3: Confirm the amount matches what you agreed off-chain.
settlement_help.funding_step_4: Click Fund — this locks your coins in escrow.
settlement_help.funding_step_5: Once the seller submits a proof, verify it and release payment.
settlement_help.proofs_body: A proof is a signed message proving delivery. The seller generates it from the Agreements page after fulfilling the obligation. The buyer reviews the proof and clicks Release to complete payment. If the proof is missing or wrong, the buyer can dispute before the deadline.
settlement_help.disputes_body: If something goes wrong, open the agreement in Agreements and click Dispute before the deadline expires. A dispute freezes the escrow. Resolution is handled on-chain by the Irium arbitration contract — both parties must submit evidence via their wallet CLI.
settlement_help.callout: Setting a deadline too short. Always give yourself enough time to complete delivery and for the buyer to review the proof. Merchant Delayed has two separate windows — the cool-down (buyer dispute period) and the total escrow window.

marketplace_help.what_body: The Marketplace is a peer-to-peer offer board. Sellers post offers describing what they're selling, at what price, and the settlement terms. Buyers browse and respond directly — no central server, orders propagate through the Irium gossip network.
marketplace_help.posting_step_1: Go to Marketplace → My Offers → click Post Offer.
marketplace_help.posting_step_2: Set the asset, price (in IRM), and settlement template.
marketplace_help.posting_step_3: Add a description so buyers know what they're getting.
marketplace_help.posting_step_4: Submit — the offer broadcasts to connected peers.
marketplace_help.responding_step_1: Browse Offers — filter by asset or price range.
marketplace_help.responding_step_2: Click an offer to view terms.
marketplace_help.responding_step_3: Click Buy — this opens a new settlement agreement pre-filled with the offer details.
marketplace_help.responding_step_4: Fund the escrow and wait for the seller to deliver and submit proof.
marketplace_help.feeds_body: Feeds are curated offer streams published by trusted sellers. Subscribe to a feed to automatically see new offers from that seller in your Browse view. Unsubscribe any time.
marketplace_help.callout: Offers are propagated via gossip — peers that go offline may not see your offer. Re-post if you haven't had a response after a few hours.

agreements_help.what_body: Agreements is your ledger of all settlement agreements you've created or participated in. You can track status, submit proofs, fund, release, or dispute from here.
agreements_help.lifecycle_step_1: Created — agreement exists on-chain, waiting for the buyer to fund.
agreements_help.lifecycle_step_2: Funded — buyer has locked coins in escrow. Seller can now fulfill and submit proof.
agreements_help.lifecycle_step_3: Proof submitted — seller has provided delivery proof. Buyer reviews.
agreements_help.lifecycle_step_4: Released — buyer approved proof, funds sent to seller. Done.
agreements_help.lifecycle_step_5: Refunded — deadline passed or dispute resolved in buyer's favour. Funds returned.
agreements_help.lifecycle_step_6: Disputed — buyer raised a dispute. Awaiting on-chain arbitration.
agreements_help.submit_step_1: Open the agreement from the list.
agreements_help.submit_step_3: Paste or upload your signed proof file.
agreements_help.importing_body: Use Import Pack to restore a batch of agreements from a JSON export. Use Import Invoice to create a new agreement directly from a payment invoice JSON generated by another Irium wallet.
agreements_help.callout: You can only submit a proof or release/refund if you are a party to the agreement and your wallet is unlocked. Make sure the correct address is selected.

reputation_help.what_body: Irium's on-chain reputation system scores addresses based on their settlement history — successful completions, disputes raised, refunds received, and how long they've been active. Scores are deterministic and verifiable by any node.
reputation_help.query_step_1: Enter a Q-prefix address or 64-hex public key in the search bar.
reputation_help.query_step_3: Review the score breakdown — overall score, completed agreements, disputes, volume.
reputation_help.tier_mid_desc: moderate history, verify before large trades.
reputation_help.tier_low_desc: use caution. Multiple disputes or refunds on record.
reputation_help.building_body: Complete agreements on time, submit proofs promptly, and avoid raising frivolous disputes. Each successfully released agreement adds to your score. Disputes lower it, even if resolved in your favour.
reputation_help.callout: Check a counterparty's reputation before funding a large escrow. A score below 60 is a red flag for first-time trades.

buyer.step_choose_subtitle: Pick the kind of trade you're looking for. We'll surface matching offers in the next step.
buyer.find_offer_hint: Find an offer from the network
buyer.review_intro: Confirm the details before taking the offer
buyer.lock_payment_hint: Lock your payment in the escrow contract
buyer.no_offers_found: No offers found. Try syncing the marketplace.
buyer.sync_to_browse: Sync the marketplace to browse available offers
buyer.select_or_enter_id: Select an offer from the marketplace, enter an offer ID, or import an offer file
buyer.funds_locked_note: Funds will be locked in a smart escrow. The seller receives payment only after submitting proof of delivery.
buyer.waiting_for_seller: Waiting for seller to deliver and submit proof

seller.step_choose_subtitle: Pick the template that best describes what you are selling. This becomes part of the offer your buyer sees.
seller.intro: Define what you're selling and the price

fields.seller_address_hint: Auto-detected from your wallet. Buyers will send payment here.
fields.expires_hint: Offer expires this many blocks from now (~10 min/block)
fields.payment_instructions_placeholder: Bank details, crypto address, or other instructions for the buyer…

seller.share_intro: Send the offer ID or file to your buyer
seller.share_buyer_note_before: Share the offer ID with your buyer. When they take the offer, an agreement is created automatically. Check your
seller.share_buyer_note_after: page for the new agreement — proof submission and release happen there.

quarantine_banner.title: Found {{count}} quarantined block file(s) from a previous session.
quarantine_banner.subtitle: These are leftover block files from a previous chain fork. Deleting them is safe — your wallet and balance are not affected, and the node will re-fetch any blocks it still needs from peers.
quarantine_banner.node_running_caption: These are leftover block files from a previous chain fork. Deleting them is safe — your wallet and balance are not affected. Stop the node first, then click Recover Now to clean them up.
quarantine_banner.toast_node_stopped: Node stopped. You can now recover the quarantined blocks.
quarantine_banner.toast_recover_partial: Recovered {{files}} file(s); some directories could not be removed: {{reason}}

titlebar.miner_active_confirm:
Mining is active. The miner will continue running in the background.

Click OK to minimize to tray, or Cancel to keep the window open and stop mining first if you want to quit completely.

notifications.agreement_satisfied: Agreement {{hash}} satisfied — funds can be released
notifications.agreement_proof_reorged: Proof for agreement {{hash}} was reorged — re-submit may be needed

terminal.page_subtitle: Restricted command runner. Whitelisted wallet and RPC commands only.
